macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Federal Reserve Cuts Rates by 0.25% Amid Government Shutdown
items.summary: The Fed lowered rates by 0.25% despite limited data due to the shutdown and ended balance sheet reduction. Two dissents occurred, with markets watching for hints on a potential December cut.

items.title: US Government Shutdown Ends with Bipartisan Deal
items.summary: Parties agreed to reopen government through January, restoring federal jobs with back pay and allowing a Senate vote on Obamacare subsidies. Trump plans legal fight over spending authority.

items.title: September CPI Shows Mixed Inflation Trends Above Fed Target
items.summary: CPI rose 0.3% overall (expected 0.4%) and core 0.2% (expected 0.3%), both up 3.0% year-over-year, exceeding the Fed's 2% target.

items.title: US Economic Data Releases: Weak Housing and Mixed Durable Goods
items.summary: Building Permits fell -10.8%, Housing Starts -3% to -5.2%, while Durable Goods Orders ex-Transport rose 0.6%; upcoming data includes ADP Employment and Jobless Claims.

items.title: Q1 GDP Data Reveals Mediocre Private Sector Growth
items.summary: Excluding government, real GDP growth was around 1.5% in Q1; recent PCE and GDP price indices show inflation at 3.1-4.5%.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Up 1.4% to $77,329 Amid Mild Bullish Trend Near $77,000
items.summary: Bitcoin fluctuated between $75,876 and $77,500 over the past 24 hours, closing up 1.4% at $77,329 as of 09:30 AM UTC. Markets show modest gains with BTC signaling a mild bullish trend near $77,000.[1][5]

items.title: Ethereum Stalls Below $2,325 with $38.2M Liquidations as ETF Outflows Pressure Demand
items.summary: Ethereum price stalled below $2,325 amid $38.2 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, including $19.5 million from short positions. Neutral momentum persists around $2,300.[2][5]

items.title: Gemini Exchange Secures Major CFTC License for Derivatives Trading
items.summary: Gemini has obtained a significant Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) license, advancing its US regulatory compliance for derivatives trading. This marks a key step in the exchange's expansion.[4]

items.title: Network-Wide Liquidations Reach $282M in Past 24 Hours, Mostly Shorts
items.summary: Total network liquidations hit $282 million over the past 24 hours, with $217 million from short positions and $65 million from longs. BTC 8-hour funding rate averages -0.0065%.[6]

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Wallet Deposits 1,744 ETH Worth $4.03M to Kraken
items.summary: An Ethereum Foundation-affiliated wallet transferred 1,744 ETH, approximately $4.03 million, to Kraken exchange. This follows reports of ETH IV declines and potential downside risks below $2,206.[6]

items.title: U.S. PCE Inflation Hits 3-Year High, Contributing to Bitcoin Slide
items.summary: U.S. PCE inflation rose to a 3-year high, pressuring Bitcoin prices amid broader market movements. BTC traded just below $78K after a 2.7% gain in 24 hours.[4][3]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google NotebookLM Major Updates: AI-Powered Learning Tutor
items.summary: Google has released significant updates to NotebookLM, transforming it into an AI-driven learning machine that reads notes, asks questions, identifies weaknesses, and teaches like a real tutor, revolutionizing productivity and information handling.

items.title: New EVoC Algorithm: 100x Speedup for Nested Clustering
items.summary: The EVoC algorithm, developed by the creators of UMAP, enables nested hierarchical clustering with a 100x speedup, making clustering far more useful for complex data analysis.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Mythos AI Model: Cybersecurity Disruptor
items.summary: Anthropic has launched Mythos, a new AI model poised to upend cybersecurity operations entirely, as discussed in the latest Reporters' Notebook series.

items.title: Andrew Ng Launches 'AI Prompting for Everyone' Course
items.summary: DeepLearning.AI released a new course by Andrew Ng teaching prompting skills to become an AI power user, covering information retrieval to app building.

items.title: Google Reveals AI Overviews and Machine Learning in Search Evolution
items.summary: Google Search engineering director discusses shift to AI Overviews, AI Mode, conversational queries, and machine learning's role in future search experiences.

items.title: Monday.com Shifts Vision to AI Agents Doing the Work
items.summary: Monday.com co-founder announces pivot from work management to AI agents performing tasks, introducing Agent Talent marketplace for hiring AI like employees in wave three of AI.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced with PS5 Demo Available Now
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announced Nioh 3 during Sony’s June State of Play, allowing soulslike fans to play a demo immediately on PS5.[2]

items.title: Bloodstained Sequel 'The Scarlet Engagement' Revealed at State of Play
items.summary: Developer ArtPlay unveiled The Scarlet Engagement, a new Bloodstained game that diverges significantly from Ritual of the Night, during the June PlayStation State of Play.[1][2]

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Confirms June 2025 Wave 1 Lineup
items.summary: Microsoft revealed the full list of games joining Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025 ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase.[2]

items.title: No More Heroes Developer Announces New Ultraviolent Sci-Fi Game
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture revealed a new over-the-top action game blending No More Heroes and Dead Rising elements.[2]

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals ‘Force of Law’ Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 announced a new Force of Law Warbond, bringing fresh content to the cooperative shooter.[2]

items.title: Kingdom Hearts IV New Screenshots Released with Unreal Engine 5 Graphics
items.summary: Square Enix shared impressive Kingdom Hearts IV screenshots using Unreal Engine 5, following the cancellation of Kingdom Hearts Missing Link.[3]

items.title: Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles Set for September Launch
items.summary: Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles is scheduled to launch in September.[1]

items.title: Dragon Ball Project: Multi Name and Network Test Dates Revealed
items.summary: The real name of Dragon Ball Project: Multi was disclosed along with dates for a new network test.[1]

items.title: Bungie Reveals Destiny 2 Roadmap with The Edge of Fate Expansion
items.summary: Bungie outlined the next year of Destiny 2 content, including The Edge of Fate and Renegades expansions.[3]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Reports Q2 Revenue Up 17% to $111.18B, China Sales Surge 28%
items.summary: Apple's quarterly revenue rose 17% year-over-year to $111.18 billion, beating estimates slightly, with net income up 19% to $29.58 billion and China revenue jumping 28% to $20.5 billion. Tim Cook noted iPhone sales were constrained by advanced chip supply issues despite strong demand.

items.title: cPanel, WHM, and WP Squared Bug Exploited Since February, CISA Urges Patch by May 3
items.summary: A critical vulnerability in popular server management software cPanel, WHM, and WP Squared, scored 9.8 on CVSS, has been exploited since February. CISA directs federal agencies to patch by May 3.

items.title: Microsoft's Xbox Mode Rolls Out to Windows 11 PCs
items.summary: Microsoft is bringing a console-like Xbox interface to Windows 11 PCs, enhancing gaming accessibility on desktops.

items.title: Anbernic Launches RG Rotate Swiveling Retro Handheld for $88 on May 11
items.summary: Anbernic's new RG Rotate retro gaming handheld with a swiveling screen starts at $88 and becomes available May 11.

items.title: Nissan Abandons Plans for US EV Plant Producing 200,000 Vehicles Yearly
items.summary: Nissan has scrapped its proposed US electric vehicle factory that was set to produce 200,000 EVs annually.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone with the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs to Challenge Microsoft and Apple
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to mainstream, aiming to extend the mobile OS to desktops and reshape personal computing.

items.title: Light Phone III to Support Curated Third-Party Apps via New SDK
items.summary: The minimalist Light Phone III will soon allow a select set of non-commercial third-party apps through an upcoming SDK for developers.

items.title: Subnautica 2 Enters Early Access on May 14 After Development Delays
items.summary: The anticipated Subnautica sequel will launch in early access on May 14 following various production challenges.
